title: Copy, Move, Remove
description: Organize a messy filesystem by moving, copying, and removing files.
scenario: A sysadmin left the server in a mess before going on leave. A critical config file is in the wrong place, old backups are cluttering the home directory, and a required data directory does not exist. Fix it all.
symptoms:
- ~/staging.conf exists in your home directory but is in the wrong place
- /etc/app/ directory does not exist
- Three backup-*.tar.gz files clutter the home directory
- /var/app/data directory is missing
mission: Move staging.conf to /etc/app/, copy it to production.conf, delete the backup files, and create /var/app/data.

hints.title: Where to look
hints.content: Run ls -l in your home directory to find staging.conf and the backup files.

hints.title: What to check
hints.content: Use mkdir -p to create /etc/app/ before you try to move the config file there.

hints.title: The fix
hints.content:
sudo mkdir -p /etc/app
mv ~/staging.conf /etc/app/staging.conf
cp /etc/app/staging.conf /etc/app/production.conf
rm ~/backup-*.tar.gz
sudo mkdir -p /var/app/data
